page: Vultr vhf-3c-8gb is an x86_64 virtual server belonging to the High Frequency Compute family. It features 3 shared Intel Core (Skylake, IBRS) physical cores running at 2.0 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.67 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 256 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results show strong performance in single-core stress-ng tests (2401.69) and top-tier single-threaded gzip compression (38.09 GB/sec). However, multi-core performance is average, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7120.84 and a weak PassMark CPU score of 5738.4. Memory performance is mixed; cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 395.59 GB/sec, but uncached large-block read and write bandwidths are weak, measuring 32.54 GB/sec and 16.94 GB/sec respectively.

The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, which is reflected in the weak multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput of 3.63 GB/sec. For AI workloads, the server handles small 135M LLM models with average efficiency but struggles with larger 7B models. This server is best suited for lightweight database operations, basic web serving, single-threaded processing tasks, and development environments that benefit from fast local NVMe storage without requiring dedicated CPU resources.
description: Vultr vhf-3c-8gb is an x86_64 virtual server in the High Frequency Compute family, configured with 3 shared Intel Skylake physical cores running at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 256 GB local NVMe SSD. While single-core performance is strong, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 2401.69 and top-tier single-threaded gzip compression, its shared CPU allocation leads to average multi-core capabilities and weak uncached memory bandwidth. The server is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that leverage its fast local NVMe storage and single-threaded performance but do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core compute. It is best suited for lightweight database operations, development environments, and single-threaded utility tasks.
og_description: Vultr vhf-3c-8gb is an x86_64 High Frequency Compute server featuring 3 shared Intel Skylake cores, 8.0 GB RAM, and a 256 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight database and web workloads.

tagline: A shared-core virtual server combining strong single-threaded performance with fast local NVMe storage for lightweight database and development workloads.

8.0 GB system memory and 256 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU and single-threaded gzip compression performance
